publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-06-25 18:00:42 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous autographs, New York State

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Yates, John V. N. (John Van Ness), 1779-1839.

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: United States. Department of the Treasury

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York (State). Commissioners of the Canal Fund

did.abstract: Letters, legal documents, and other official communications, mainly concerning business transactions with signatures.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Theodore Sedgwick served variously as a diplomat to the Netherlands, Assistant Secretary of State under President Buchanan, and U.S. District Attorney in New York state. Frank Hiscock served as a Congressman and then a Senator from New York before returning to practice law in Syracuse. Joel Benton was a journalist and poet. Elbert Hubbard was a farmer, businessman, writer, magazine publisher, and founder of the Roycroft Community in East Aurora, New York. William Sulzer served as a lawyer, state legislator, and governor of New York.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Letters, legal documents, and other official communications, mainly concerning business transactions. Documents include indentures conveying plots of land to new owners (1798 and 1845), the will of New York City merchant William Constable (1817), a butcher's license to operate in Albany (1823), receipts for purchases (1833), appointment of a deacon for the Methodist Episcopal Church in Watertown (1836), an appropriation of funds to Abraham V. Putnam from the Commissioners of the Canal Fund for enlargement of the Erie Canal (1843), a commission for a colonel in the state militia (1844), official communications from the U.S. Treasury (1861, 1865), and a trademark registration from the U.S. Patent Office (1907). Letters discuss politics, social and speaking engagements, and family news. Also included is a circular from the headquarters of the Republican County Committee (1878), signed by Frank Hiscock, asking the recipient to send in a list of names of local "luke-warm Republicans, doubtful Democrats or Labor Reformers" so that they can be sent "some sound Republican newspaper" until the next election. Correspondents include John V. N. Yates, George W. Clinton, Daniel D. Bernard, U.S. Treasurer Francis Elias Spinner, Albert Bierstadt (who signs a letter written by his wife Rosalie, 1874), Theodore Sedgwick, Unitarian minister Samuel J. May, Columbia University president Nicholas Murray Butler, Theodore Roosevelt (writing as Assistant Secretary of the Navy, 1898), Joel Benton, Elbert Hubbard (writing in 1909), and William Sulzer, among others.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Miscellaneous Autographs, #120. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: United States -- Politics and government -- 19th century.
New York (State) -- Politics and government -- 19th century.
New York (State) -- Social life and customs.

unittitle.extref: Link to a PDF finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Jonathan Eames to Reuben Hoyt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jonathan Eames, Als, Newtown to Reuben Hoyt of Newtown, Queens County

did.unittitle: Indenture between David and Catherine Fonda and Hugh Harsha

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Indenture between David and Catherine Fonda of Albany, Albany County and Hugh Harsha of Saratoga County, Mohawk River, to sell piece of land in Saratoga County.

did.unittitle: Jacob Kingsbury to Asa Spaulding

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jacob Kingsbury, Als, Buffalo Creek, Erie County to Asa Spaulding, Norwich Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Frederick Edwards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Frederick Edwards, legal document, Jefferson County; Robert Breckenridge

did.unittitle: James Caldwell, Als
Peter Schermerhorn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peter Schermerhorn, record of merchandise imported from England with his autograph.

did.unittitle: Determaination of Controversies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An award on Determaination of Controvrsies between Hugh Staats and Isaac Hempsted of Albany, Albany County; Arbitrators, James Lanrange and Hugh Boyd

did.unittitle: Protest of non-acceptance of Bill of Exchange

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Protest of non-acceptance of Bill of Exchange by Francis March against Anthony Stafford, New York City. Egbert Benson, Notary Public.

did.unittitle: Receipt for money

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Francis I. Bloodgood against Polydore G. B. Wisner. Receipt for money to be paid to plaintiff written by J. C. Spencer, Attorney for defendant.

did.unittitle: William Hunter to Hermann Bleecker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Hunter, Als. Newport, Herkimer County, to Hermann Bleecker, counsellor at law, Albany, Albany County, requesting professional aid for a Mr. Whitehouse.

did.unittitle: iel Low to Augustus Fleming

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Niel Low, Als, Ballston Spa to Augustus Fleming, New York City.

did.unittitle: William Constable's Will

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of will of William Constable made January 25, 1803 and recorded in the Surrogate's office in New York on May 17, 1817

did.unittitle: J. G. Hale to Edward Cuth Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J. G. Hale, Als, Rochester, Monroe County to Edward Cuth Jr. concerning the hearing of a case.

did.unittitle: W. Van Ness to John V. Henry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: W. Van Ness, Als, New York to John V. Henry, Albany, Albany County, recommending Mr. Henry W. Livingston.

did.unittitle: J. V. N. Yates to J. V. Henry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J. V. N. Yates, distinguished lawyer and writer on law. 2 Ls. First, Albany, Albany County to J. V. Henry and second, to Mr. Olcott

did.unittitle: Corporation notice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Corporation notice to John Townsend from George B. Smith, collector, Albany, Albany County.

did.unittitle: Butcher license

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Patrick Cassidy, license as a butcher, signed by Charles E. Dudley, Mayor, Albany, Albany county.

did.unittitle: Sereno Dwight to Edward D. Griffin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sereno E. Dwight, Als, Boston, to Rev. Edward D. Griffin, requesting Griffin to preach in Boston, Mass.

did.unittitle: Ogden Edwards to John Todd

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ogden Edwards, Als, New York City to John Todd requesting aid in obtaining appointment to Supreme Court.

did.unittitle: C. Jenkins

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: C. Jenkins, Als, Saratoga Springs, Saratoga County, relating to his health.

did.unittitle: Ebenezer Emmons to Webster and Skinner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ebenezer Emmons, Geologist, physician, professor, Als, Castleton, Vermont to Messrs. Webster and Skinner ordering manual of mineralogy.

did.unittitle: George W. Clinton to Chief Justice Hitchcock

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: George W. Clinton, son of DeWitt Clinton, Als, to Chief Justice Peter Hitchcock, asking whether he can be admitted to the bar in Ohio.

did.unittitle: William Gaffer receipt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Gaffer, receipt, Albany, Albany County for $5 received from Peter Bane to pasture one cow for the season.

did.unittitle: Peter Bain

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peter Bain, bill and receipt from Jefferson Mayele, per William Mayele.

did.unittitle: Levi Woodbury to William L. Marcy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Levi Woodbury, Secretary of the Treasury, Ls., to William L. Marcy, Governor of New York, Albany

did.unittitle: Appointment of Lorenzo D. Tanner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appointment of Lorenzo D. Tanner as deacon by Beverly Waugh, Bishop of Methodist Episcopal Church, Watertown, Jefferson County.

did.unittitle: R. Davis note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: R. Davis note for $300, Salina, Onondaga County, payable to John Phillips at Dutchess County Bank.

did.unittitle: Thomas Morris to Joseph B. Boyd

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thomas Morris, Als, New York City to Joseph B. Boyd, Cincinnati, Ohio, sending one of the late Capt. Jos. Branatt's letters.

did.unittitle: Daniel Dewey Barnard to Thurlow Weed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Daniel Dewey Barnard, Als, Washington, D. C. to Thurlow Weed, relating to a speech and political matters.

did.unittitle: William C. Preston to Mr. Rhodes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William C. Preston, Als, Washington, D. C. to Mr. Rhodes about Judge Bay. Presumably William Campbell Preston (1794-1860), South Carolina Senator, 1833-1842, not his cousin William Preston, (1816-1887). KJ

did.unittitle: Gerritt Smith, Als.
John Bartell to Rev. J. P. B. Storer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Bartell, Als, Buffalo, Erie County to Rev. J. P. B. Storer.

did.unittitle: Coree Vanderden to George W. Kirtland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Coree Vanderden, Als, Coeymans, Albany County to George W. Kirtland, Waterford, Saratoga County, relating to rent accounts.

did.unittitle: A note to be paid

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A note by George W. Little, Canal Commissioner, Albany, Albany County to be paid to Abraham N. Putnam.

did.unittitle: Warrant to John Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Warrant issued to John Friend, electing him corporal by Colonel Ezra Miller, Fort Hamilton, Kings County.

did.unittitle: Indenture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Indenture between Charles Butler, Franklin Dexter, John B. Jones and Erastus Corning, trustees of the American Land Co., and John Stewart of Waterford, Saratoga County for tract of land in Oak Orchard, Orleans County .

did.unittitle: Certificate of baptism of John Barstow Willard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Samuel J. May, Minister of Church of Messiah, Syracuse, Onondaga County. Certificate of baptism of John Barstow Willard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: S. A. Foot, Als, Geneva, Ontario County to A. W. Harvey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: M. Wheeler, Als, Richfield Springs, Otsego County to James Dexter about lots in Milford, Otsego County.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William H. Bogert, Als, Albany, Albany County to J. B. Ruggles, New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Archibald Anderson, Als, Johnstown, Fulton County to James Dexter about tax bill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sophia Wright, Als, Addison, Steuben County

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promissory note for Thurlow Weed and E. E. Kendrik Albany, Albany County to pay E. Whitehouse

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: General Francis E. Spinner to Phillips, Owen, Hazlett, Washburn, Foster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four letters from General Francis E. Spinner, (4) Als, (2) Ls, Washington, D. C. to D. Phillips, Henrietta, N. Y. about savage's pension bill; to Mr. Owen, deposit letter; to C. A. Hazlett, Portsmouth N. H. about Fractional Currency; to W. W. Washburn, East Hampton, Mass.; to G. B. Foster, Yonkers, Westchester County

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: W. B. Sprague, Als, Albany, Albany County to Rev. Jenks thanking him for a book and an article.
